commonAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to all deployed objects
commonAnnotations.title: commonAnnotations

commonLabels.description: Labels to add to all deployed objects
commonLabels.title: commonLabels

configMapAnnotations.description: Additional annotations for the ConfigMap objects. Evaluated as a template
configMapAnnotations.title: configMapAnnotations

configMapLabels.description: Additional labels for the ConfigMap objects. Evaluated as a template
configMapLabels.title: configMapLabels

enabled.description: Conditional property used in parent chart
enabled.title: enabled

exampleValue.description:
# bitnami/common
# It is required by CI/CD tools and processes.
#
#
exampleValue.title: exampleValue

extraDeploy.description: Array of extra objects to deploy with the release
extraDeploy.title: extraDeploy

global.description:
Values in the .global section can be accessed by both parent and child subcharts as
.Values.global.*: https://helm.sh/docs/chart_template_guide/subcharts_and_globals/

imageCredentials.description:
Optional credentials to log in and fetch images from a private registry. These credentials
are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentials.title: imageCredentials

imageCredentialsSecrets.description:
Optional list of existing Secrets containing image pull credentials to use for pulling
images from private registries. These Secrets are shared with all the subcharts automatically
imageCredentialsSecrets.title: imageCredentialsSecrets

ingress.description:
Ingress defaults shared across the parent chart and all subcharts. Each subchart's local
`ingress.*` value takes precedence when set; otherwise the global is used.

annotations.description:
Annotations merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.annotations` wins on key collision.
Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

defaultPathType.description:
Default path type applied to ingress rules when `ingress.defaultPathType` is not set.
`Prefix` works for nginx, traefik, AWS ALB, and most modern controllers.
defaultPathType.title: defaultPathType

enabled.description:
Enable Ingress for this chart. OR'd with the chart's local `ingress.enabled` so
setting this once at the parent enables all subchart Ingresses.
enabled.title: enabled

extraLabels.description:
Extra labels merged into the Ingress. Local `ingress.extraLabels` wins on key collision.
Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

ingressClassName.description: Default ingress class name applied when `ingress.ingressClassName` is not set
ingressClassName.title: ingressClassName

path.description:
Default path applied to ingress rules when `ingress.path` is not set.
AWS ALB users should override to `/*`.
path.title: path

tls.description: TLS entries concatenated with the local `ingress.tls`. Evaluated as a template
tls.title: tls

ingress.title: ingress

platformServiceAddress.description:
Seqera Platform Service name: can be the internal Kubernetes hostname or an external ingress
hostname. Evaluated as a template.
Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent
`platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServiceAddress.title: platformServiceAddress

platformServicePort.description:
(int) Seqera Platform Service port.
Required when deploying this subchart standalone. When deploying as part of the parent
`platform` umbrella chart, this value is inherited from the parent chart's `global` section
platformServicePort.title: platformServicePort

studiosConnectionUrl.description:
Base URL for Studios connections: can be any value, since each session will use a unique
subdomain under `.global.studiosDomain` anyway to connect. Evaluated as a template
studiosConnectionUrl.title: studiosConnectionUrl

studiosDomain.description:
Domain where the Studios service listens. Make sure the TLS certificate covers this and its
wildcard subdomains. Evaluated as a template
studiosDomain.title: studiosDomain

global.title: global

annotations.description: Ingress annotations specific to your load balancer. Evaluated as a template
annotations.title: annotations

defaultBackend.description:
Configure the default service for the ingress (evaluated as template)
Important: make sure only one defaultBackend is defined across the k8s cluster: if the
ingress doesn't reconcile successfully, 'describe ingress <name>' will report problems
defaultBackend.title: defaultBackend

defaultPathType.description: Default path type for the Ingress. When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.defaultPathType`
defaultPathType.title: defaultPathType

extraHosts.description: Additional hosts you want to include. Evaluated as a template
extraHosts.title: extraHosts

extraLabels.description: Additional labels for the ingress object. Evaluated as a template
extraLabels.title: extraLabels

ingressClassName.description:
Name of the ingress class (replaces the deprecated annotation `kubernetes.io/ingress.class`).
When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.ingressClassName`
ingressClassName.title: ingressClassName

path.description: Path for the main ingress rule. When empty, falls back to `global.ingress.path`
path.title: path

tls.description: TLS configuration. Evaluated as a template
tls.title: tls

enabled.description:
Enable init containers that coordinate startup dependencies (for example, wait for Seqera
Platform readiness before starting, etc)
enabled.title: enabled

enabled.description: Enable wait for Seqera Platform init container before starting the proxy
enabled.title: enabled

extraEnvVars.description: Additional environment variables for the init container
extraEnvVars.title: extraEnvVars

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the init container (e.g. to mount a CA certificate)
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

digest.description: Wait for Platform init container image digest in the format `sha256:1234abcdef`
digest.title: digest

pullPolicy.description: imagePullPolicy for the wait for Platform init container
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

registry.description: Wait for Platform init container image registry
registry.title: registry

repository.description: Wait for Platform init container image repository
repository.title: repository

tag.description: Wait for Platform init container image tag
tag.title: tag

resources.description: Container requests and limits for different resources like CPU or memory
resources.title: resources

capabilities.description: Fine-grained Linux kernel privileges to add or drop for the container
capabilities.title: capabilities

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem read-only to prevent in-place writes or tampering
readOnlyRootFilesystem.title: readOnlyRootFilesystem

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root UID (prevents starting if UID 0)
runAsNonRoot.title: runAsNonRoot

runAsUser.description: UID the container processes run as (overrides container image default)
runAsUser.title: runAsUser

proxy.description: Options for the Studios Proxy component

args.description: Override default container args (useful when using custom images)
args.title: args

command.description: Override default container command (useful when using custom images)
command.title: command

capabilities.description: Fine-grained Linux kernel privileges to add or drop for the container

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mounts the container root filesystem read-only to prevent in-place writes or tampering
readOnlyRootFilesystem.title: readOnlyRootFilesystem

runAsGroup.title: runAsGroup

runAsNonRoot.description: Boolean that requires the container to run as a non-root UID (prevents starting if UID 0)
runAsNonRoot.title: runAsNonRoot

runAsUser.title: runAsUser

extraEnvVars.title: extraEnvVars

extraEnvVarsCMs.title: extraEnvVarsCMs

extraEnvVarsSecrets.title: extraEnvVarsSecrets

extraOptionsSpec.description:
Extra options to place under .spec (e.g. replicas, strategy, revisionHistoryLimit, etc).
Evaluated as a template
extraOptionsSpec.title: extraOptionsSpec

extraOptionsTemplateSpec.description:
Extra options to place under .spec.template.spec (e.g. nodeSelector, affinity, restartPolicy,
etc). Evaluated as a template
extraOptionsTemplateSpec.title: extraOptionsTemplateSpec

extraVolumeMounts.description:
List of volume mounts to add to the container (evaluated as template). Normally used with
`extraVolumes`
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

extraVolumes.description:
List of volumes to add to the deployment (evaluated as template). Requires setting
`extraVolumeMounts`
extraVolumes.title: extraVolumes

digest.description: Proxy container image digest in the format `sha256:1234abcdef`
digest.title: digest

pullPolicy.description:
imagePullPolicy for the Proxy container
Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#pre-pulled-images
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

pullSecrets.description:
List of imagePullSecrets
Secrets must be created in the same namespace, for example using the .extraDeploy array
Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/
pullSecrets.title: pullSecrets

initContainers.description: Additional init containers for the proxy pod. Evaluated as a template
initContainers.title: initContainers

localCacheTTL.description: TTL for local cache of Redis keys used for resiliency against Redis failures
localCacheTTL.title: localCacheTTL

oidcClientRegistrationToken.description:
Initial access token to share with Seqera Platform to restrict registration requests to
only authorized OIDC clients. The token can be provided as a string of random chars or as an
external k8s Secret: in the latter case, a key can also be provided. If neither a string nor a
Secret is provided, the chart will generate a random token
WARNING: Always explicitly set this value or use an existing secret when using Kustomize.
Auto-generated random values are incompatible with Kustomize. When upgrading releases via
Kustomize, Helm cannot query the cluster to check if a secret already exists, causing it to
regenerate a new random value on each upgrade
oidcClientRegistrationToken.title: oidcClientRegistrationToken

oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretKey.description: Key in the existing Secret containing the OIDC client registration token
oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretKey.title: oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretKey

oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretName.description:
Name of an existing Secret containing the OIDC client registration token
as an alternative to the oidcClientRegistrationToken field. Note: the Secret must already exist
in the same namespace at the time of deployment
oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretName.title: oidcClientRegistrationTokenSecretName

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations for the proxy pod. Evaluated as a template
podAnnotations.title: podAnnotations

podLabels.description: Additional labels for the proxy pod. Evaluated as a template
podLabels.title: podLabels

podSecurityContext.description:
Configure Pods Security Context.
Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod
Empty map to disable Pod Security Context configuration

fsGroup.description:
Sets the GID that Kubernetes will apply to mounted volumes and created files so processes
in the pod can share group-owned access
fsGroup.title: fsGroup

resources.title: resources

extraOptions.title: extraOptions

nodePort.description:
Service node port, only used when service.type is Nodeport or LoadBalancer
Choose port between 30000-32767, unless the cluster was configured differently than default
nodePort.title: nodePort

targetPort.description:
Port on the pod/container that the Service forwards traffic to (can be a number or
named port, distinct from the Service's external port)
targetPort.title: targetPort

type.description: Proxy Service type. Note: ingresses using AWS ALB require the service to be NodePort
type.title: type

serviceAnnotations.title: serviceAnnotations

serviceLabels.description: Additional labels for the Service objects. Evaluated as a template
serviceLabels.title: serviceLabels

proxy.title: proxy

redis.description:
Seqera Studios requires a Redis cache. Seqera recommends using a managed Redis installation
provided by an external provider.

enableTls.description: Enable TLS when connecting to Redis
enableTls.title: enableTls

existingSecretKey.description: Key in the existing Secret containing the password for Redis
existingSecretKey.title: existingSecretKey

existingSecretName.description:
Name of an existing Secret containing credentials for Redis, as an alternative to the
password field. Note: the Secret must already exist in the same namespace at the time of
deployment
existingSecretName.title: existingSecretName

password.description: Redis password if the installation requires it
password.title: password

prefix.description: Key prefix to use when storing Studios sessions in Redis
prefix.title: prefix

server pods, which will be discovered by DNS and contacted directly, not through the Service
